title: Auto Populate Column Attached to Mirrors Using Data from Another Board

posts.author: RossMcQuillan (Ross McQuillan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working with connected boards and could use some guidance on automations. Here’s my setup:

  1. I have a board called Hub Articles 2024, which includes a text column named Submitter Name.

    image1294×504 86.4 KB

  2. There’s an existing automation in Hub Articles 2024 that creates a new item in a second board, L2 Articles 2024, but only if the item belongs to a specific group (L2).

  3. L2 Articles 2024 is connected to three mirrored columns that pull information relevant to the Submitter Name.

    L2 Articles 20241204×241 24.9 KB

Here’s the issue: while the automation creates the new item in L2 Articles 2024, it doesn’t populate the Submitter Name column in this board. I’d like to know if there’s a way to automatically populate the Submitter Name column in L2 Articles 2024 with data from the corresponding column in Hub Articles 2024 when the new item is created.

Is this possible within the existing automations, or would I need to set up an additional integration or use a workaround?

Automation1184×470 24.3 KB

Thanks in advance for any help or advice!

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
It seems odd that it isn’t already working… Is it a two way connect column? I think you may need another automation. Type in match in automation template center to see your options. You will need to match the items somehow though with data from a column.

Bruce Gosk | bruce@mintconsulting.org
Book in here
+61 447 126 859
Mint Consulting Australia | Certified monday.com Silver Partner

posts.author: RossMcQuillan (Ross McQuillan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Bruce - thanks for coming back to me.

It’s an awkward one because information is essentially being duplicated from the board in the first screenshot to the board in the second. The Submitter Name field in the second screenshot is connected to and pulling information from a different Team List board. When a user enters a name in the Submitter Name field, it gives the option to select a team member from a dropdown, henceforth populating the columns Title, Team and Email in that second screenshot.

The awkwardness is such that the two Submitter Name fields, one in each of the first two screenshots, are different column types; one being a linked column and one being a plain text column. As there are two different boards to reference from L2 Articles 2024 (team list and hub articles 2024), the Submitter Name column won’t connect to both.

I imagine that there may be a much better way of doing it than I have figured thus far so any inspiration is appreciated. In the meantime, I will check out the match automation.

Thanks again!

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: If you can’t push the data from one board to another with an extra column, or use a match automation, there may be a solution using make.com or zapier.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Ross, how did you go? Did you try make or zapier? Let me know if you need any help. You can Book in here for a free consultation (for anything monday related).

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @RossMcQuillan

You can now auto populate connected columns using the Connect Sync app. The app keeps your connect columns automatically updated when a new item is added, deleted or moved to the specified group(s) or board(s).

If you have any questions concerning the app, you can write to support@kolaai.com

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Of course @RossMcQuillan ,

Try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

Centralize your designs (7)1920×960 218 KB

image1676×728 35.3 KB

We have a feature that lets you auto-fill the connect board columns. It also keeps the items completely synced so any change on the original item will also reflect on the connected item.

You can also setup an the same automation you currently have with our app, the advantage being that the items will mirror, link and be completely in sync (incl. updates + replies).
